reasoning: Based on the most recent October 2025 polling data before the knowledge cutoff, Johannes Kaiser is polling at 11.95%, placing him in 4th position behind Jeannette Jara (29.54%), José Antonio Kast (23.26%), and Evelyn Matthei (15.9%). The difference between Kaiser and frontrunner Jara is nearly 18 percentage points. Even accounting for historically observed polling errors in Chile (5+ percentage points), the gap is too large to close in the remaining two weeks of campaigning. Kaiser would need an unprecedented polling error combined with a massive shift in voter preference.
